steps.title: Find the shared critique
steps.content: Both texts argue that scholarship on modernist fiction is too narrow. Text 1 says fragmented narratives receive too much attention while coherent works such as Elizabeth von Arnim's are neglected. Text 2 says the focus on fragmentation versus coherence blocks inquiry into other stylistic similarities.

steps.title: Reject claims only one text supports
steps.content: Choice B invents a goal of rehabilitating reputations and frames the issue as unproductive debate. Choice C depends on computer technology, which appears only in Text 2 and is not described as making older methods obsolete.

steps.title: Reject the style reversal
steps.content: Choice D says scholarship must move beyond style, but both texts are concerned with style. Text 1 compares fragmented and coherent narrative structures, and Text 2 wants more stylistic correspondences studied, not less attention to style.

steps.title: Choose the shared warning
steps.content: Both authors would agree that without a broader focus, scholarship will keep presenting an unnecessarily limited view of modernist fiction in Britain and Ireland. The answer is A.
